payload.source_text: The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Source elements present in the ledger.

original_descriptions: Enable children to claim and track completed pocket money tasks on a weekly basis
Provide children visibility into available and completed task inventory
Weekly cycle governing earned amount computation and presentation
Weekly cycle resetting task availability state

payload.evaluated_scope: All input material in this project

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL ZachmanCell elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Pocket money task completion record
payload.justification: Task is the central entity referenced across signals for claiming, defining, tracking, and viewing | Signals reference completed tasks as distinct strategic entity requiring tracking and visibility

payload.description: Weekly earnings summary
payload.justification: Earnings summary is a distinct entity of strategic importance for child motivation

payload.description: Child performance report
payload.justification: Performance report is a strategic entity required for parental compensation decisions

payload.description: Longitudinal task history
payload.justification: Task history is a strategic entity supporting accountability and trend analysis

payload.description: Monetary value associated with task
payload.justification: Signal identifies monetary compensation as a strategic attribute of task assignments

payload.description: Weekly cycle for task completion tracking and earnings calculation
payload.justification: Both signals explicitly reference weekly periodicity for task tracking and earnings summary

payload.description: Child user claiming and tracking pocket money tasks
payload.justification: Multiple signals reference children as primary actors performing task claiming, tracking, and earnings viewing

payload.description: Parent user defining tasks and accessing performance reports
payload.justification: Signals identify parents as actors responsible for task definition and performance reporting access

payload.description: Enable children to view available tasks and track completed pocket money tasks on a weekly basis
payload.justification: Signal explicitly expresses fundamental purpose of task claiming and tracking for children at strategic level | Signal captures strategic purpose of task visibility for child users

payload.description: Enable parents to define compensated task assignments with monetary values
payload.justification: Signal articulates parental motivation for task definition and compensation management

payload.description: Enable children to view their weekly earnings summary
payload.justification: Signal identifies motivational goal for earnings visibility at strategic level

payload.description: Enable parents to access child performance reporting for accurate compensation distribution
payload.justification: Signal establishes parental need for performance data supporting compensation decisions

payload.description: Provide stakeholders access to longitudinal task history for accountability and trend analysis
payload.justification: Signal indicates strategic requirement for historical data access supporting accountability objectives

payload.description: Children claim completed tasks for monetary reward tracking
payload.justification: Signal SG290 establishes the core business process of children claiming completed tasks for reward tracking.

payload.description: Parent specifies tasks with monetary values
payload.justification: Signal SG291 defines the parent capability to specify task entities with associated monetary value - business function.

payload.description: Weekly aggregation of child earnings
payload.justification: Signal SG294 establishes weekly aggregation capability for earned monetary totals - distinct business function.

payload.description: Parent reporting of child-task-earnings relationships
payload.justification: Signal SG295 defines parent reporting capability to support payment fulfillment - business function.

payload.description: Task availability resets at weekly boundaries
payload.justification: Signal SG296 specifies the cyclical business rule for task availability reset at weekly temporal boundaries.

payload.description: Historical task data must be retained and accessible
payload.justification: Signal SG297 mandates retention and accessibility of historical task data - business rule.

payload.description: Monetary value entity associated with tasks
payload.justification: Signal SG291 explicitly defines parent capability to specify task entities with monetary value attributes - clear entity with attribute. | Signal SG294 establishes weekly aggregation capability for earned monetary totals - derived data entity.

payload.description: Child actor entity
payload.justification: Signals SG292 and SG293 reference child entities in relationships and visibility requirements - distinct business entity.

payload.description: Task entity with status tracking
payload.justification: Multiple signals reference completed tasks as distinct entities linked to children and visible in the system. | Signal SG293 defines visibility requirements for available task entities within temporal scope.

payload.description: Historical task data
payload.justification: Signal SG297 mandates retention of historical task data entities across temporal periods.

payload.description: Child task relationship/linkage
payload.justification: Signal SG292 explicitly specifies the relationship between completed task entities and child entities through linkage. | Signal SG295 defines parent reporting capability displaying child-task-earnings relationships.

payload.description: Weekly business cycle
payload.justification: Signals SG294 and SG296 establish weekly aggregation and reset cycles - recurring business cycle.

payload.description: Task completion event
payload.justification: Signals reference completed tasks and claiming process - business event triggering state change.

payload.description: Weekly boundary trigger for task availability reset
payload.justification: Signal SG296 specifies task availability reset at weekly temporal boundaries - temporal trigger.

payload.description: Parent role
payload.justification: Signals SG291 and SG295 define parent capabilities (task specification, reporting) - distinct business role.

payload.description: Child role
payload.justification: Multiple signals reference child actors claiming tasks and viewing data - distinct business role.

payload.description: Track monetary rewards earned by children for completed tasks
payload.justification: Signal SG290 establishes the core business concept of reward tracking - primary business goal.

payload.description: Support parent payment fulfillment to children
payload.justification: Signal SG295 defines reporting capability to support payment fulfillment - business goal.

payload.description: Child actors must have visibility of available and completed tasks within temporal scope
payload.justification: Signal SG293 defines child actor visibility requirements - business constraint on system behavior.

payload.description: Weekly earnings totals must be visible to child actors
payload.justification: Signal SG294 establishes visibility requirement for weekly totals - business constraint.

payload.description: Historical task data must be retained across temporal periods
payload.justification: Signal SG297 mandates retention of historical data - business constraint on data management.

payload.description: Access control function enforcing child user visibility to task collections
payload.justification: Signal specifies data visibility requirements, implying a logical function that controls access patterns for child users.

payload.description: Weekly aggregation process computing earned amounts for children
payload.justification: Signal describes temporal aggregation logic for computing and presenting weekly earned amounts, representing a logical process.

payload.description: Reporting function aggregating child earnings and task completion data for parents
payload.justification: Signal describes a logical reporting interface that provides aggregated views, representing a reporting function.

payload.description: Weekly state reset process restoring task availability
payload.justification: Signal defines periodic state reset logic as part of a weekly cycle, representing a logical process that operates on task availability.

payload.description: Association between task completion event and child entity
payload.justification: Signal explicitly describes a logical association rule linking task completion events to child entities, representing a relationship in the data model.

payload.description: Task collection accessible to child users
payload.justification: Signal describes data visibility requirements for task collections, implying task collection as a logical entity with access patterns.

payload.description: Weekly earned amount computed for child entity
payload.justification: Signal describes temporal aggregation logic for earned amounts, representing a derived attribute associated with child entities computed on a weekly basis.

payload.description: Child earnings data aggregated for parental view
payload.justification: Signal describes aggregated child earnings data as part of a reporting interface, representing a logical entity or view combining earnings and completion data.

payload.description: Historical task record persisted for retention
payload.justification: Signal mandates logical persistence and accessibility of historical task records, representing a retained data entity.

payload.description: Task completion event triggering association with child entity
payload.justification: Signal describes an association rule triggered by task completion events, representing a logical event in the system.

payload.description: Weekly cycle governing earned amount computation, presentation, and task availability state reset
payload.justification: Signal defines weekly temporal aggregation logic, representing a recurring weekly cycle for computation. | Signal explicitly defines weekly cycle behavior for periodic state reset, representing a recurring temporal cycle.

payload.description: Child user role with access to task collections
payload.justification: Signal specifies access patterns for child users, implying a logical role with defined visibility permissions.

payload.description: Parent role viewing aggregated child earnings and task data
payload.justification: Signal describes a reporting interface for parents, implying a logical role with access to aggregated child data.

payload.description: Historical task records must be logically persisted and accessible
payload.justification: Signal mandates data retention requirements, representing a design constraint on logical persistence and accessibility.

payload.description: Operating system version compatibility constraint for platform deployment
payload.justification: Signal explicitly describes a platform compatibility constraint specifying concrete OS versions at the physical/technical level, directly mapping to technical constraint in Why column.

payload.description: Weekly earned amount access timing specification
payload.justification: SG542 establishes weekly operational schedule boundary for earned amount access; at Row 5 this is a detailed timing specification for the weekly access cycle.

payload.description: Weekly task availability reset timing specification
payload.justification: SG544 specifies weekly operational cycle timing for task reset; this is a detailed sequencing specification for the weekly refresh cycle.

payload.description: iOS mobile platform deployment node
payload.justification: SG545 explicitly identifies iOS as a named deployment platform; each named platform requires a separate Node CCI per Rule 5.

payload.description: Android mobile platform deployment node
payload.justification: SG545 explicitly identifies Android as a named deployment platform; each named platform requires a separate Node CCI per Rule 5.

payload.description: Windows desktop platform deployment node
payload.justification: SG545 explicitly identifies Windows as a named deployment platform; each named platform requires a separate Node CCI per Rule 5.

payload.description: Child user interface for task claiming operations
payload.justification: SG539 identifies children as operational actors executing task claiming activities; at Row 5 this translates to the detailed user interface specification for child actor interactions.

payload.description: Parent user interface for task configuration and value assignment operations
payload.justification: SG540 identifies parents as operational actors for task configuration and monetary assignment; at Row 5 this represents the detailed interface specification for parent actor functions.

payload.description: Child user interface for viewing available and completed tasks within weekly cycles
payload.justification: SG541 defines operational visibility requirements for child actors viewing tasks; this is a detailed interface specification for child user read-access patterns.

payload.description: Parent user interface for accessing earned amount reports and distribution activities
payload.justification: SG543 defines parent operational role in report access and pocket money distribution; at Row 5 this represents the detailed interface for parent reporting and payment functions.

payload.title: High Level Description
payload.description: Section: High Level Description

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Segment elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Expresses the fundamental purpose of enabling children to claim and track completed pocket money tasks weekly.
Articulates parental motivation to define compensated task assignments with associated monetary values.
Captures the purpose of providing children visibility into available and completed task inventory.
Identifies the motivational goal of enabling children to view their weekly earnings summary.
Establishes parental need to access child performance reporting for accurate compensation distribution purposes.
Indicates stakeholder requirement for longitudinal task history access supporting accountability or trend analysis.
Section header indicating the commencement of conceptual system description.
Establishes the core business concept of children claiming completed tasks for monetary reward tracking.
Defines the parent capability to specify task entities with associated monetary value attributes.
Specifies the relationship between completed task entities and child entities through linkage.
Defines child actor visibility requirements for both available and completed task entities within temporal scope.
Establishes weekly aggregation capability for earned monetary totals visible to child actors.
Defines parent reporting capability displaying child-task-earnings relationships to support payment fulfillment.
Specifies the cyclical business rule for task availability reset at weekly temporal boundaries.
Mandates retention and accessibility of historical task data entities across temporal periods.
Logical association rule defining how task completion events are linked to child entities.
Data visibility requirements specifying logical access patterns for child users to task collections.
Temporal aggregation logic defining weekly computation and presentation of earned amounts to children.
Logical reporting interface providing parents aggregated view of child earnings and task completion data.
Periodic state reset logic defining weekly cycle behavior for task availability restoration.
Data retention requirement mandating logical persistence and accessibility of historical task records.
Platform compatibility constraint specifying concrete operating system versions for deployment.
Identifies children as operational actors who execute task claiming activities throughout the week on a temporal basis.
Identifies parents as operational actors responsible for task configuration and monetary value assignment activities.
Defines operational visibility requirements for child actors viewing available and completed tasks within weekly cycles.
Establishes weekly operational schedule boundary when child actors can access earned amount totals.
Defines parent operational role in accessing reports to execute pocket money distribution activities.
Specifies weekly operational cycle timing when task availability resets for claiming operations.
Defines deployment platform locations specifying where the system operates across iOS, Android, and Windows environments.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Signal elements present in the ledger.
